nativeRange.habitat: Subtidal coral reef zones and rubble areas; tends to grow anchored to hard substrate or rubble in moderate water movement.

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Eucheuma isiforme; sole hobby trade source for this species.

summary: Spiny Seaweed (Eucheuma isiforme) is a rare display-grade red macroalga with stiffly branched, spiny fronds and striking coloration that can range from deep red to orange or yellow depending on collection origin and tank conditions. It is seldom available in the hobby trade and is considered one of the more demanding macroalgae to keep.
careNotes: Eucheuma isiforme is a red alga (Rhodophyta, family Solieriaceae) despite trade names that may suggest otherwise. Color is highly variable — specimens may be red, orange, or yellow depending on collection location and tank conditions. As a red alga, E. isiforme must NOT be buried in sand; burying the base will cause it to melt. It is generally advised to attach it to live rock or rubble using wedging (positioning between rocks) or zip-tie/fishing line; superglue has been documented to cause base melt in some macroalgae and should be used with caution. Moderate, indirect flow is preferable — too much direct current can damage the spiny thallus. Full-spectrum LED lighting is recommended; actinic-only spectrum is generally considered insufficient for more demanding red macroalgae species. Weekly dosing of iron, iodine, and trace micronutrients is generally advised. Target water chemistry: temperature 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, nitrate 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm. Herbivorous tankmates — including tangs, rabbitfish, and sea urchins — have been known to consume this alga. A sibling entry, Green Spiny Seaweed (sw-algae-050, Eucheuma sp.), represents the unidentified green-coloration form of this genus sold separately.
breedingNotes: Aquarium propagation is typically by fragmentation — detaching healthy branch segments and allowing them to anchor to rubble or rock. Sexual reproduction (gametophyte/sporophyte alternation) has not been documented in home aquaria.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby trade source; confirmed species identity as Eucheuma isiforme, coloration variability (red/orange/yellow), care level (difficult), and shipping sensitivity requiring Next-Day Air.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source. Confirmed water parameters (temp 22–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, Ca 400–450 ppm, Mg 1250–1350 ppm, NO3 5–20 ppm, PO4 0.02–0.1 ppm), propagation guidance (wedging preferred, zip-tie/fishing line acceptable), red-algae sand-bed melt warning, full-spectrum lighting requirement, and weekly micronutrient dosing recommendation.
